start.title: The Forest's Edge
start.text: My legs feel heavy as I stand at the forest's edge. The trees ahead look wrongtoo tall, their branches twisted together like arthritic fingers reaching for something that isn't there. Barely any light makes it through. There's a path, I guess, worn down by... something. And these flowers to my left, they're glowing. Actually glowing. Pulsing like they're breathing. I can feel my heart pounding in my chest. Should I really be here?

choices.text: Follow the worn path deeper into the forest
[BRAVE] Charge straight into the darkest part of the forest
Turn back - this place feels too dangerous

deep_forest.title: Deep in the Forest
deep_forest.text: I'm in too deep now. The trees press in from all sides, and I swear I can hear breathing that isn't mine. There's a cave up aheadits entrance looks like a throat, dark and wet. Something about it makes my skin crawl. The wooden path off to the side looks safer, but... safer doesn't always mean better, does it? My hands are shaking.

choices.text: [REQUIRES COURAGE: 50] Enter the cave despite every instinct screaming no
Go back to the forest edge while I still can

magic_flowers.title: The Glowing Flowers
magic_flowers.text: The flowers pulse with this weird bioluminescent lightblue, then purple, then back again. As I get closer, I feel warmth spreading through my chest. It's not painful, more like... relief? Like when you finally exhale after holding your breath too long. My wounds ache less. I can feel my heartbeat slowing down to something almost normal.

choices.text: Go back to the forest edge
[HEALTHY] Pick some flowersmight need them later

safe_retreat.title: Safe Retreat
safe_retreat.text: You decide it's too dangerous and turn back. You leave the forest safely.

cliff_edge.title: The Cliff Edge
cliff_edge.text: I wasn't paying attention. One second I'm walking, the nextnothing under my feet. The fall feels like it lasts forever. Then impact. God, the impact. I can't breathe. Can't think. When I finally look down, I wish I hadn't. My leg is bent the wrong way, white bone jutting through the skin, blood pooling around a jagged rock that's... that's inside me. In my side. I can feel it. Every breath makes it worse. This is how I die, isn't it? Not in some heroic way. Just... stupid. So stupidly.

choices.text: [REQUIRES COURAGE: 60] Bite down and try to pull myself together
Scream for helpI don't care about pride anymore

cliff_suffer.title: Agonizing Pain
cliff_suffer.text: There's so much blood. It's warm, sticky, spreading across the rocks. Every time I try to move, the rock shifts inside me and I taste copper in my mouth. I think I'm crying but I can't tell if it's tears or sweat. The pain is white-hot, consuming everything. Please. Please just make it stop. I've never felt anything like this. Never imagined I could hurt this much and still be conscious.

choices.text: [REQUIRES COURAGE: 70] Force myself to move, to climb
I'm losing too much blood... getting cold...

cliff_endure.title: Enduring the Fall
cliff_endure.text: You crawl painfully, but the cliff is steep. You slip further.

cliff_climb.title: Climbing Back
cliff_climb.text: You grab rocks, but they crumble. You fall again, harder.

death_cliff1.title: Death by Fall
death_cliff1.text: My body hits somethinganother ledge maybe. I feel my ribs cave in with this wet crunch. Can't breathe. Trying to breathe. Blood fills my throat, I'm choking on it, drowning in my own blood. My vision's narrowing, just a pinpoint now. I can feel my heart hammering, frantic, then... slower. Slower. This wasn't supposed to happen. I didn't wantI didn'teverything's fading. The last thing I feel is my own blood, still warm, spreading beneath me. Then nothing.

cliff_help.title: Calling for Help
cliff_help.text: You shout for help. A shadowy figure appears above.

cliff_plead.title: Pleading
cliff_plead.text: The figure laughs and pushes a boulder. It rolls towards you.

cliff_dodge.title: Dodging
cliff_dodge.text: You dodge, but slip and fall further into a pit of spikes.

cliff_rope.title: The Rope
cliff_rope.text: The figure throws a rope, but it's too short. You grab it, swing, and crash into rocks.

cliff_swing.title: Swinging
cliff_swing.text: You swing into a tree, branches pierce your body.

death_cliff2.title: Brutal Cliff Death
death_cliff2.text: Spikes and branches tear through you. Blood sprays, intestines spill out. You scream as vultures peck at your exposed guts while you're still alive, slowly eating you from the inside out. Agony lasts hours until your heart finally stops. Horrific.

cave_entrance.title: The Cave Entrance
cave_entrance.text: The cave walls aren't stonethey're organic, pulsing. Flesh-like. I can feel warmth radiating from them and my stomach turns. Three passages branch off. The left one has what looks like teeth carved into the walls, jutting inward. The middle passage... I can hear dripping, wet sounds echoing from deep inside. And the rightchittering. Clicking. The unmistakable sound of too many legs moving across stone. Something about this whole place feels like a trap. Like I've walked into something's mouth.

choices.text: [REQUIRES COURAGE: 55] The teeth hallwaymaybe they're just carvings
The wet roomwater might mean a way out
[REQUIRES COURAGE: 70] The spider chamberface it head-on
Back out of the cavethis was a mistake

teeth_hallway.title: The Teeth Hallway
teeth_hallway.text: The teeth aren't carvings. They're real. I can see them flexing, grinding against each other with this horrible scraping sound. The walls pulse rhythmicallybreathing. The whole corridor is breathing. One of the teeth snaps at me as I pass and I jerk back, feeling the air move past my face. If I'd been an inch closer...

choices.text: [REQUIRES COURAGE: 65] Sprint through before they close
Back away to the cave entrance

teeth_run.title: Running Through Teeth
teeth_run.text: I run. God, I run. But they're fast. One catches my shoulderrips through my jacket, through skin, takes a chunk of muscle with it. Another clips my thigh. I feel hot blood running down my leg. Keep moving. Don't stop. Don't think about the pain. Just keepthere! An opening ahead!

teeth_escape.title: Teeth Escape
teeth_escape.text: I made it through, but I'm leaving a blood trail. My hands are shaking so badly I can barely stand. And then I see it. Ahead. A mouth. Not teeth-lined wallsan actual mouth. Massive. Open. Waiting. The throat disappears into darkness that seems to move, to writhe. I can smell it from hererotting meat and bile. This is what the hallway was feeding into. This is what it wanted to deliver me to.

teeth_crawl.title: Crawling Through
teeth_crawl.text: Slow. Stay low. I press myself against the fleshy floor, trying not to think about how warm it is, how it yields slightly under my weight. Most of the teeth miss me. Most. One catches my arm, punctures straight through. I bite my lip so hard I taste blood, trying not to scream. If I scream, I'll attract attention. If I attract attention...

teeth_continue.title: Deeper Crawl
teeth_continue.text: The hallway narrows, teeth close in.

death_teeth.title: Death by Teeth
death_teeth.text: The mouth doesn't swallow quicklyit savors. I feel myself sliding down the throat, walls of muscle contracting around me, squeezing. The teeth start their work slowly. Methodical. First, I feel my ribs crack, one by one, like someone snapping dry twigs. Then my spinethat's worse. So much worse. I can't move my legs. Can't move anything below my neck but I can still feel it. All of it. The digestive acid starts seeping in, burning through my clothes first, then skin. It's not quick. My nerve endings are still firing, sending signals of agony that my brain can't process fast enough. I'm being dissolved. Melted. I can feel my flesh liquefying, sloughing off bones that are themselves beginning to soften, to dissolve. Days pass. Or hours. Or minutes. Time has no meaning in this darkness, this pain. I'm still conscious. Still aware. Still feeling every moment as my body stops being a body and becomes nothing but scattered molecules in digestive fluid. This is dying. Truly dying. Not the quick snap of the neck or fade to black. This is being unmade, piece by piece, cell by cell, while something in me that should have died hours ago stubbornly refuses to let go.

wet_room.title: The Wet Room
wet_room.text: Water. Actual water. The cavern opens into a natural pool, clear and still. I can see my reflection on the surfaceI look worse than I thought. The water looks inviting. Cold. Clean. Maybe it could help with the wounds. But something about it feels off. Too still. Too clear. Nothing grows in it. No algae, no plants, nothing.

choices.text: Cup some in my hands and drink
Wade in deepermaybe there's an exit underwater
Back away from the watertrust my instincts

wet_drink.title: Drinking the Water
wet_drink.text: It tastes amazing. Sweet, almost honey-like. I feel energy coursing through me immediately, wounds tingling as they start to close. But thensomething else. A pressure in my stomach. Growing. My skin feels tight. Too tight.

wet_swim.title: Swimming Deeper
wet_swim.text: The water's temperature changes as I go deeper. Warm. Warmer. Hot. Oh god, it's getting hot. It's boiling. My skinI can feel blisters forming, bursting. The pain is instant and all-consuming.

death_wet.title: Death by Water
death_wet.text: My body swells like a balloon. I can feel my skin stretching, stretching, hear it creaking. Then the first rupturea small tear that becomes a gaping split. Blood and fluids spray out. Or the boiling water peels my skin away in sheets, exposing raw red muscle that immediately blisters white. I try to swim up but I can't tell which way is up anymore. My eyes boil in their sockets. I feel them burst. I'm still conscious, still aware when I feel the first wriggling thing enter my mouth. Parasites. The water is full of them. They pour into me through every orifice, every wound. I can feel them moving. Inside me. Laying eggs in my intestines, my stomach, burrowing through soft tissue. The eggs hatch. Larvae. Hundreds of them. Thousands. Eating me from the inside out. I can feel each one. Feel them crawling through my veins, my arteries, bursting out through my skin in eruptions of blood and writhing white bodies. I should be dead. Why am I not dead? Why can I still feel this? Days pass. My consciousness shouldn't exist anymore but it does, trapped in this dissolving meat, feeling every larvae tunnel, every bite, every

spider_chamber.title: The Spider Chamber
spider_chamber.text: The clicking stops the moment I enter. That's worse. The silence is so much worse. Then movementin the corners, on the ceiling, dropping from above. Spiders. But not normal ones. These are massive, each one the size of a large dog, maybe bigger. Their eyes reflect my torch lighttoo many eyes, all focused on me. Mandibles clicking. I can see the hairs on their legs, thick as needles. One of them hisses. I didn't know spiders could hiss.

choices.text: [REQUIRES COURAGE: 75] FightI won't die without trying
Run back to the cave entrance
I can't move. My legs won't work. I can't

spider_fight.title: Fighting Spiders
spider_fight.text: I swing wildly, connecting with one. It splits open, spraying green ichor. But there are so many more. I feel a sting on my legfangs. Venom pumps into me. It burns like liquid fire traveling up my veins. Another sting. Another. They're everywhere.

spider_run.title: Running from Spiders
spider_run.text: You run into a web trap.

death_spider.title: Death by Spiders
death_spider.text: The webbing is sticky, impossibly strong. More of it wraps around me with each passing second until I'm completely encased. I can't move. Can't even blink. Then the paralytic venomI feel the fangs sink deep into my neck. The poison spreads fast. My body goes numb butoh godI can still feel. I can feel everything, I just can't move, can't scream, can't do anything but lie here and experience what comes next. They start with my legs. I watchbecause I can't close my eyesas they inject digestive enzymes. My flesh begins to liquefy from the inside out. They slurp it up. The sounds. Those horrible slurping, sucking sounds. They move up my body. Patient. Methodical. My arms next. Then my torso. I can feel my organs turning to liquid, being sucked out through holes they pierce in my skin. My heart's still beating. I can feel it hammering in my chest, pumping blood that just leaks out into their waiting maws. Hours. This goes on for hours. Days maybe. Time stops meaning anything. All that exists is the sensation of being eaten alive, unable to scream, unable to die, just... consumed. Piece by piece. Sip by sip. Until there's nothing left that could be called human.

wooden_path.title: The Wooden Path
wooden_path.text: The path is sturdy and leads to a peaceful glade. You find some gold coins. A traveling merchant offers to guide you safely for 30 gold.

choices.text: [REQUIRES GOLD: 30] Hire the merchant as guide

glade_rest.title: Peaceful Rest
glade_rest.text: For the first time since entering this nightmare forest, I feel almost safe. The glade is quiet. Genuinely quiet, not that oppressive silence that makes you think something's watching. I can hear birds. Normal birds. My body aches, begging me to rest.

choices.text: Let myself fully relaxjust for a moment
[HEALTHY] Get up and keep moving while I still can
Head back down the wooden path

glade_relax.title: Relaxing
glade_relax.text: I close my eyes. Just for a second. That's all I need, just one second ofpain. Sharp, burning pain in my calf. My eyes snap open and I see it coiled there, a snake, fangs still embedded in my flesh. Its eyes are cold, reptilian. It doesn't let go. Just keeps pumping venom into me.

glade_wake.title: Waking Up
glade_wake.text: The poison works fast. My muscles start seizing. I can't control my own body anymore. Convulsions rack through me, so violent I feel bones crack. Foam bubbles from my mouth. I bite my tongue nearly in half. Blood everywhere. Can't breathe right. Can't breathe at all.

death_glade.title: Death by Poison
death_glade.text: The neurotoxin paralyzes my diaphragm. I'm suffocating, drowning in air I can't breathe. My eyes bulge from the pressure. Blood vessels burstin my eyes, my nose, my ears. I can feel my internal organs shutting down one by one. Liver first, then kidneys. My blood stops clotting properly, starts pooling internally. The seizures continue even as my brain begins to shut down from lack of oxygen. Each convulsion breaks another boneI hear my ribs snap, my arm, my collarbone. The pain is astronomical but I can't even scream. Then my heart. I feel it stutter, skip a beat. Skip two. Try to restart and fail. Try again. The muscle fibers tear themselves apart trying to beat with no coordination. And finally, mercifully, it just... stops. Everything stops. Except my consciousness, which lingers for seconds, minutes, hours in the void, still processing the phantom sensations of a body that's no longer functioning.

path_continue.title: Deeper Woods
path_continue.text: The path leads to ruins. Old. Really old. The stones are covered in moss and carvings I don't recognize. In the center, something glintsgold? Artifacts? The whole place has this aura of forbidden knowledge, of things that were buried for a reason.

choices.text: Search the ruinsfortune favors the bold

ruins_investigate.title: Ancient Ruins
ruins_investigate.text: I step inside and immediately hear clickingmechanical. Traps. Of course there are traps. In the far corner sits a chest, ancient but intact. On the walls, inscriptions in a language that hurts to look at. The letters seem to writhe, to whisper. And that artifact on the pedestalit's pulsing with a sickly green light.

choices.text: [BRAVE] Grab the treasure chest and run

death_ruins.title: Death by Curse/Demons
death_ruins.text: The curse takes hold immediately. I watch in horror as my skin begins to necrotize, turning black and sloughing off in wet chunks. But I don't die. The curse won't let me die. I remain conscious, aware, trapped in this rotting meat prison as my flesh peels away to reveal bone, as my organs liquefy and leak out. Or the demonsthey're real, they're so horribly real. Claws like razors flay my skin in surgical strips. They're methodical, keeping me alive, keeping me aware. They pour molten metal into the wounds and the painthe pain transcends anything physical. It's agony given form, stretched across an eternity. My screams echo through dimensions I can't comprehend. Days become weeks become years. Time loses all meaning. My body is nothing but a husk of suffering, but my consciousness remains, trapped, experiencing every second of torment, unable to die, unable to escape. This is worse than death. This is forever.

safe_victory.title: Victorious Escape!
safe_victory.text: Through your courage, cunning, and resourcefulness, you've managed to escape the Enchanted Forest alive! Your bravery will be remembered in legends. Gold: {gold}, Courage: {courage}, Health: {health}
